prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response: {"wallet_id":"a3f7c1d209e4b856","BT":2,"BW":4,"HF":0.38,"RF":0.27,"MA":1,"hour_entropy":2.15,"behavioral_consistency":0.13,"response_latency_variance":7.22,"action_sequence_perplexity":1.65,"error_recovery_pattern":0.065,"gas_nonce_gap_regularity":0.78,"eip1559_tip_precision":0.39,"gas_price_precision":0.61,"rationale":"Seed 1032 profiles a time-habitual user (~-1.3σ hour_entropy) with erratic inter-tx cadence and below-average gas precision, placing all 5 HasciDB indicators safely under flag thresholds while sitting in the lower tails of several human AI-feature distributions to avoid cross-axis leakage."}
